titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the E. F. Benson Papers
titlestmt.author: by Will Baker

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 7, 2018 at 04:45

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The E. F. Benson Papers consist of writings, correspondence, and business and financial files, documenting Benson's fiction-writing career and spanning the years 1894 to 1929. Writings consist almost entirely of drafts, most corrected, of short stories by Benson, his novel Paul (1906), and an evidently unpublished and untitled novel written in 1906. Among the short stories are two of Benson's most celebrated "spook stories," "The Superannuation Department AD 1945" (1906) and "The Thing in the Hall" (1912). Other papers include correspondence, sales and royalty statements, contracts, bills, and receipts that document Benson's business dealings with his literary agency (the Authors' Syndicate, Ltd.) and various publishers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David J. Holmes on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, the Starling W. Childs Library Fund, and the George Henry Nettleton Fund, 1991-1996, and from Julian Nangle on the George Henry Nettleton Fund, 1997.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The E. F. Benson Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: E. F. Benson Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Uncat.MS Vault.File, Uncat.MS Vault.632, Uncat.MS Vault.634, and Uncat.MS Vault.705.

bioghist.head: E. F. Benson, 1867-1940
bioghist.p: Edward Frederic Benson was a British author known most widely today for his comic "Mapp and Lucia" series and his short stories of the supernatural. E. F. Benson was the fifth child of Mary Sidgwick Benson and Edward White Benson, who served as Archbishop of Canterbury from 1883 to 1896. Benson was educated at Marlborough College and King's College, Cambridge, where he completed his studies in 1892.

Benson's first novel, Dodo , was published in 1893 and enjoyed significant acclaim and commercial success. Benson went on to a prolific literary career, publishing nearly 100 works including novels, short stories, memoirs, biographies, and other works of non-fiction. Benson also worked as an archaeologist at excavations in Italy, Greece, and Egypt, represented England in international figure skating competitions, and served in the 1930s as the mayor of the town of Rye in East Sussex.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The E. F. Benson Papers consist of writings, correspondence, and business and financial files, documenting Benson's fiction-writing career and spanning the years 1894 to 1929. Writings consist almost entirely of drafts, most corrected, of short stories by Benson, his novel Paul (1906), and an evidently unpublished and untitled novel written in 1906. Among the short stories are two of Benson's most celebrated "spook stories," "The Superannuation Department AD 1945" (1906) and "The Thing in the Hall" (1912). Other papers include correspondence, sales and royalty statements, contracts, bills, and receipts that document Benson's business dealings with his literary agency (the Author's Syndicate, Ltd.) and various publishers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings, 1894-1924. II. Correspondence, 1901-1929. III. Business and Financial Papers, 1902-1918.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with some other material arranged at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists chiefly of drafts of writings by E. F. Benson, including 41 short stories, the novel Paul , one untitled and evidently unpublished novel, and a review of "Jérusalem" by Pierre Loti. A large portion of the drafts are corrected in the hand of the author or others. Unless otherwise indicated, all materials are holograph or typescript drafts (original or carbon).

did.unittitle: "Arturo's Boat" (clipping of published copy on newsprint)
"The Heroic Georgie" (clipping of published copy on newsprint)
"The Superannuation Department, A.D. 1945" (carbon typescript and galley proofs)
"The Tragedy of Bertram Porter (A Warning to Faddists)"
Manuscript addition to an unknown story, with carbon typescript notes entitled, "Stories for the Screen"
Opinion, evidently to a publisher, by Walter de la Mare on E. F. Benson's unpublished sequel to "Dodo" (presumably "Dodo's Daughter," published in 1913). In carbon of typescript signed in type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of incoming and outgoing correspondence of the literary agency, The Authors' Syndicate, Ltd., relating to the writings of E. F. Benson. Correspondents include E. F. Benson, William M. Colles, William Heinemann, and other agents, editors, and publishers. Correspondence consists of holograph and typescript letters (most signed), retained copies of letters in secretarial hands, carbon copies of letters, secretarial notes, and telegrams.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Publishers' papers arranged alphabetically by publisher, followed by general papers arranged alphabetically by genre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of this series comprises British and American publishers' royalty statements, sales reports, and account records relating to E. F. Benson's works, consisting of printed forms completed in type or manuscript. Additional materials largely relate to the Authors' Syndicate literary agency and include bills (primarily for typing and copying services), contracts with publishers, receipts from publishers, and account statements.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous lists, notes, records, and ephemera.
